zh.lifeSummary: Joan de Guernsey於大約1268年出生在根西島。她於1297年與Sir Helias De Aubeney在英國林可爾史爾結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於大約1324年在英國林可爾史爾素斯 英格萊比享年58歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Joan de Guernsey于大约1268年出生在根西岛。她于1297年与Sir Helias De Aubeney在英国林可爾史爾结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于大约1324年在英国林可爾史爾素斯 英格萊比享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Guernsey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): habitational name from the island of Guernsey in the Channel Islands. The name of the island is from the Old Norse ey ‘island’, with an uncertain first element, perhaps the male personal name Grani in the genitive case with original -ar, or from a genitive case form of grǫn ‘pine’. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Gurnsey
meanings: English (southern): apparently an altered form of   Guernsey  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: Joan de Guernsey was born about 1268, in Guernsey. She married Sir Helias De Aubeney in 1297, in Lincolnshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died about 1324, in South Ingleby, Lincolnshire, England, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Joan de Guernsey est née environ 1268, à Guernesey. Elle a épousé Sir Helias De Aubeney en 1297, à Lincolnshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1324, à South Ingleby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Joan de Guernsey wurde um 1268 in Guernsey geboren. Sie hat 1297 in Lincolnshire, England Sir Helias De Aubeney geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb um 1324 im Alter von 58 Jahren in South Ingleby, Lincolnshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Joan de Guernsey è nata il circa 1268 nel Guernsey. Lei sposato Sir Helias De Aubeney nel 1297, nel Lincolnshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì circa 1324, nel South Ingleby, Lincolnshire, England, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Guernseyは約1268年にガーンジーで生まれた。1297年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアでSir Helias De Aubeneyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1324年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアソウス イングレビーで58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Guernsey(은)는 대략 1268년 건지에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1297년 영국 링코실르에서 Sir Helias De Aubeney(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1324년 영국 링코실르 소우스 잉글레비에서 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Joan de Guernsey nasceu aproximadamente 1268, em Guernsey. Ela casou-se com Sir Helias De Aubeney em 1297, em Lincolnshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu aproximadamente 1324, em South Ingleby, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Joan de Guernsey родилась около 1268 г. в местности Гернси. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Helias De Aubeney в 1297 г. в местности линколншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла около 1324 г. в местности соут инглеби, линколншир, Англия в возрасте 58 лет.
